attempts.strategy: Show the D(r)-component c satisfies c.hom ∘ₗ toOpen.hom = ρ_r; both toOpen.hom (Mathlib instance) and ρ_r (keystone) are IsLocalizedModule (powers r) of Γ(X,F), so c.hom is the canonical linearEquiv between the two localizations, hence bijective
attempts.code_tried: have hcomp' : c.hom ∘ₗ toOpen.hom = ρ_r := by rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]; exact congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom hcomp; set e := IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv ...; have hce : c.hom = e.toLinearMap := by apply IsLocalizedModule.ext ... hcl.map_units; ext x; simp only [he, LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearEquiv.coe_coe, IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv_apply]; rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]; change Function.Bijective ⇑c.hom; rw [hce]; exact e.bijective
